response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries which became the starting point for the group’s gathering. David collected 15 more than Emily meaning he had 39 blueberries in total. This number also represented three times what Sophia had so Sophia ended up with 13 blueberries. Together they pooled all their blueberries to use for making muffins.

***

The total number of blueberries collected by Emily David and Sophia added up to 76. Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries so they divided the total by 9 to find the number of batches they could make. This calculation showed that they could prepare 8 full batches with 4 blueberries left over which were not enough for another batch. Each batch was sold for 5 dollars which meant they had a total revenue from the muffins.

***

The revenue from the muffins was calculated by multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch. This gave them a total amount of 40 dollars from selling all the muffins they could make. They used all the blueberries they collected and turned them into a profitable product through their baking efforts. The process involved careful planning and teamwork to maximize their resources.

***

Their collaboration resulted in a successful outcome where they made money from their blueberries. The experience taught them the value of working together and using available resources wisely. They learned that even small amounts can add up when used effectively. The journey of collecting making and selling muffins was a valuable lesson in youth.
